calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment; Declaration Of Matthew Sirolly In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-06-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Matthew N. Sirolly, Esq. of the California Department of Industrial Relations, T:213.897.1511, for plaintiff/moving party, on Court Call;
Marc Libarle, Esq. of the LAW OFFICE OF MARC LIBARLE, T:415.928.2400, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: Matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF LABOR COMMISSIONER OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Entry Of Judgment as follows:

Plaintiff's motion for entry of judgment is granted as follows. Defendants are ordered to pay $800,000 plus interest and to execute a deed of trust as to the property at 819 Ellis Street, and are prohibited from classifying their drivers as independent contractors. Rather than seek judgment in the form of a deed of trust on the property, plaintiff seeks judgment in the form of a court ordered sale, which goes beyond what was agreed to. "Although a judge hearing a [Code of Civil Procedure] section 664.6 motion may receive evidence, determine disputed facts, and enter the terms of a settlement agreement as a judgment?nothing in section 664.6 authorizes a judge to create the material terms of a settlement, as opposed to deciding what terms the parties themselves have previously agreed upon." (J.B.B. Inv. Partners, Ltd. v. Fair (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 974, 983.)

Defendants' other arguments are rejected. Defendants do not show mutual mistake. A "mistake of fact is a mistake, not caused by the neglect of a legal duty on the part of the person making the mistake, and consisting in?An unconscious ignorance or forgetfulness of a fact past or present, material to the contract." (CCP 1577.) Defendants do not show that they were ignorant or forgetful of the existence of the abstracts of judgment. Indeed, defendants have a petition in this very court challenging the validity of the citations the Labor Commissioner recorded the abstracts to recover.

Defendants' constitutional arguments are meritless, and defendants fail to disclose clear contrary authority. "The Division of Labor Standards Enforcement (DLSE) is the state agency authorized to enforce California's labor laws." (Gattuso v. Harte-Hanks Shoppers, Inc. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 554, 563.) The "Constitution's jury trial provision does not operate to preclude administrative adjudication." (McHugh v. Santa Monica Rent Control Bd. (1989) 49 Cal.3d 348, 386.) "By itself, the combination of investigative, prosecutorial, and adjudicatory functions within a single administrative agency?does not violate the due process rights of individuals." (Morongo Band of Mission Indians v. State Water Res. Control Bd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 731, 737.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
